1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead

20	import (
21		"bufio"

37	)

39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
90		if backendType == "" {
91			backendType = configBackend
92		}
93		if backendType == "" {
94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}
96	
97		s := &Store{
98			Collection: DefaultCollection,
99			Dir:        dir,
100			Prefix:     prefix,
101			LockWait:   parseDurationOr("DDX_BEAD_LOCK_TIMEOUT", 10*time.Second),
102		}
103		for _, opt := range opts {
104			if opt != nil {
105				opt(s)
106			}
107		}
108		spec := DefaultRegistry().Resolve(CollectionID(s.Collection))
109		s.File, s.LockDir = spec.PathsUnder(dir)
110	
111		// Set up external backend if configured. bd/br lack per-collection
112		// scoping in their CLI, so non-default collections (e.g. beads-archive)
113		// route through a JSONL fallback the external backend transparently
114		// delegates to. The default collection always goes straight to bd/br
115		// to preserve the existing interchange contract (schema_compat_test.go,
116		// import/export round-trip).
117		switch backendType {
118		case BackendBD, BackendBR:
119			var fallback RawBackend
120			if s.Collection != DefaultCollection {
121				fallback = NewJSONLBackend(s.Dir, s.File, s.LockDir, s.LockWait)
122			}
123			if ext, err := NewExternalBackendWithFallback(backendType, s.Collection, fallback); err == nil {
124				s.backend = ext
125			}
126			// Fall through to JSONL if tool not available
127		case BackendAxon:
128			s.backend = NewAxonBackend(s.Dir, s.LockWait)
129		}
130	
131		return s
132	}

139	// Init creates the storage directory and file.
140	func (s *Store) Init(ctx context.Context) error {
141		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
142			return err
143		}
144		if s.backend != nil {
145			return s.backend.Init()
146		}
147		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
148			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init dir: %w", err)
149		}
150		f, err := os.OpenFile(s.File, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
151		if err != nil {
152			return fmt.Errorf("bead: init file: %w", err)
153		}
154		return f.Close()
155	}

157	// GenID generates a unique bead ID with the configured prefix.
158	func (s *Store) GenID(ctx context.Context) (string, error) {
159		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
160			return "", err
161		}
162		prefix := s.Prefix
163		if prefix == "" {
164			prefix = DefaultIDPrefix
165		} else if !strings.HasSuffix(prefix, "-") {
166			prefix += "-"
167		}
168		return RandomHexIDGenerator{Prefix: prefix, Bytes: 4}.GenID(ctx)
169	}

171	// ReadAll loads all beads from the configured backend.
172	func (s *Store) ReadAll(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error) {
173		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
174			return nil, err
175		}
176		if s.backend != nil {
177			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
178			if err != nil {

182		}
183		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
184		if err != nil {
185			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
186		}

188		for _, warning := range warnings {

190		}
191		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
192			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
193			if repairErr != nil {
194				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
195			}
196			if repaired {
197				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
198			}
199		}
200		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
201			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
202		}
203		return beads, nil
204	}
205	
206	// ReadAllFiltered loads all beads, folds them by latest-wins, and returns only
207	// those for which the predicate returns true. When the predicate is nil every
208	// bead is returned (equivalent to ReadAll). The predicate is applied at the
209	// per-entry parse boundary after fold — matched beads are appended directly to
210	// the return slice without first being held in an intermediate full-corpus
211	// list, so queries that match a small subset avoid materializing the
212	// mismatches (ddx-9ce6842a Part 2 step 2: filter pushdown).
213	func (s *Store) ReadAllFiltered(ctx context.Context, pred func(Bead) bool) ([]Bead, error) {
214		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
215			return nil, err
216		}
217		if s.backend != nil {
218			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
219			if err != nil {
220				return nil, err
221			}
222			if pred == nil {
223				return all, nil
224			}
225			out := make([]Bead, 0, len(all))
226			for _, b := range all {
227				if pred(b) {
228					out = append(out, b)
229				}
230			}
231			return out, nil
232		}
233		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
234		if err != nil {
235			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %w", s.File, err)
236		}
237		beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
238		for _, warning := range warnings {
239			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, warning)
240		}
241		if len(warnings) > 0 && len(beads) > 0 {
242			repaired, repairErr := s.repairJSONL()
243			if repairErr != nil {
244				return beads, fmt.Errorf("bead: repair %s: %w", s.File, repairErr)
245			}
246			if repaired {
247				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: repaired %s; backup written to %s.bak\n", s.File, s.File)
248			}
249		}
250		if len(beads) == 0 && len(warnings) > 0 {
251			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: read %s: %d malformed record(s), 0 valid", s.File, len(warnings))
252		}
253		if pred == nil {
254			return beads, nil
255		}
256		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(beads))
257		for _, b := range beads {
258			if pred(b) {
259				out = append(out, b)
260			}
261		}
262		return out, nil
263	}
264	
265	func (s *Store) readAllRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
266		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
267		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
268			return nil, nil, nil
269		}
270		if err != nil {
271			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("read: %w", err)
272		}
273		beads, warnings, err := parseBeadJSONL(data)
274		if err != nil {
275			return nil, nil, err
276		}
277		return beads, warnings, nil
278	}
279	
280	func parseBeadJSONL(data []byte) ([]Bead, []string, error) {
281		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader(data))
282		// 16MB max line. Real-world extremes observed: 1.46MB bead rows when a
283		// reviewer stream dumped into events[].body, and bd-exported lines stacking
284		// many 65KB fields. bd's upstream per-field cap is 65,535 bytes; an
285		// individual bead with dozens of maxed-out fields fits comfortably in 16MB.
286		scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 16*1024*1024)
287	
288		var beads []Bead
289		var warnings []string
290		lineNo := 0
291		for scanner.Scan() {
292			lineNo++
293			line := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())
294			if line == "" {
295				continue
296			}
297			b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
298			if err != nil {
299				warnings = append(warnings, fmt.Sprintf("bead: read line %d: %v", lineNo, err))
300				continue
301			}
302			beads = append(beads, b)
303		}
304		if err := scanner.Err(); err != nil {
305			return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("scan jsonl: %w", err)
306		}
307		return beads, warnings, nil
308	}
309	
310	func (s *Store) repairJSONL() (bool, error) {
311		var repaired bool
312		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
313			beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
314			if err != nil {
315				return err
316			}
317			beads = foldLatestBeads(beads)
318			if len(warnings) == 0 || len(beads) == 0 {
319				return nil
320			}
321			backupPath := s.File + ".bak"
322			backupData, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
323			if err != nil {
324				return fmt.Errorf("read current file: %w", err)
325			}
326			if err := writeAtomicFile(backupPath, backupData); err != nil {
327				return fmt.Errorf("write backup: %w", err)
328			}
329			if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
330				return fmt.Errorf("write repaired file: %w", err)
331			}
332			repaired = true
333			return nil
334		})
335		return repaired, err
336	}
337	
338	func foldLatestBeads(beads []Bead) []Bead {
339		if len(beads) == 0 {
340			return nil
341		}
342	
343		latest := make(map[string]Bead, len(beads))
344		lastSeen := make(map[string]int, len(beads))
345		for i, b := range beads {
346			latest[b.ID] = b
347			lastSeen[b.ID] = i
348		}
349	
350		ids := make([]string, 0, len(latest))
351		for id := range latest {
352			ids = append(ids, id)
353		}
354		sort.Slice(ids, func(i, j int) bool {
355			if lastSeen[ids[i]] != lastSeen[ids[j]] {
356				return lastSeen[ids[i]] < lastSeen[ids[j]]
357			}
358			return ids[i] < ids[j]
359		})
360	
361		out := make([]Bead, 0, len(ids))
362		for _, id := range ids {
363			out = append(out, latest[id])
364		}
365		return out
366	}
367	
368	func (s *Store) readAllLatestRaw() ([]Bead, []string, error) {
369		// When a non-JSONL backend is configured, the raw bead corpus lives
370		// outside .ddx/<collection>.jsonl entirely (axon: two collections under
371		// .ddx/axon/; bd/br: an external tool's store). Delegate the read so
372		// every read-modify-write path inside Store stays consistent with the
373		// backend's storage layout. Backends are responsible for returning
374		// already-folded beads.
375		if s.backend != nil {
376			all, err := s.backend.ReadAll()
377			if err != nil {
378				return nil, nil, err
379			}
380			return all, nil, nil
381		}
382		beads, warnings, err := s.readAllRaw()
383		if err != nil {
384			return nil, nil, err
385		}
386		return foldLatestBeads(beads), warnings, nil
387	}

389	// tmpPath returns a unique temp file path in the same directory as path.
390	// Uses pid + 4 random bytes so concurrent processes don't collide.
391	func tmpPath(path string) (string, error) {
392		b := make([]byte, 4)
393		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
394			return "", err
395		}
396		return fmt.Sprintf("%s.tmp-%d-%s", path, os.Getpid(), hex.EncodeToString(b)), nil
397	}
398	
399	func writeAtomicFile(path string, data []byte) error {
400		tmp, err := tmpPath(path)
401		if err != nil {
402			return err
403		}
404		f, err := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_WRONLY|os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL, 0o644)
405		if err != nil {
406			return err
407		}
408		if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
409			f.Close()
410			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
411			return err
412		}
413		if err := f.Sync(); err != nil {
414			f.Close()
415			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
416			return err
417		}
418		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
419			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
420			return err
421		}
422		if err := os.Rename(tmp, path); err != nil {
423			_ = os.Remove(tmp)
424			return err
425		}
426		return nil
427	}

429	func normalizeCollection(name string) string {
430		name = strings.TrimSpace(name)
431		if name == "" {
432			return DefaultCollection
433		}
434		name = strings.TrimSuffix(name, ".jsonl")
435		return name
436	}

438	// WriteAll writes all beads to the configured backend, sorted by ID.
439	func (s *Store) WriteAll(beads []Bead) error {
440		sort.Slice(beads, func(i, j int) bool {
441			return beads[i].ID < beads[j].ID
442		})
443	
444		if s.backend != nil {
445			return s.backend.WriteAll(beads)
446		}
447	
448		if err := os.MkdirAll(s.Dir, 0o755); err != nil {
449			return fmt.Errorf("bead: mkdir: %w", err)
450		}
451	
452		tmp, err := tmpPath(s.File)
453		if err != nil {
454			return fmt.Errorf("bead: tmp name: %w", err)
455		}
456		f, err := os.OpenFile(tmp, os.O_WRONLY|os.O_CREATE|os.O_EXCL, 0o644)
457		if err != nil {
458			return fmt.Errorf("bead: create tmp: %w", err)
459		}
460	
461		enc := json.NewEncoder(f)
462		enc.SetEscapeHTML(false)
463		for _, b := range beads {
464			data, err := marshalBead(b)
465			if err != nil {
466				f.Close()
467				os.Remove(tmp)
468				return fmt.Errorf("bead: marshal: %w", err)
469			}
470			if _, err := f.Write(data); err != nil {
471				f.Close()
472				os.Remove(tmp)
473				return fmt.Errorf("bead: write: %w", err)
474			}
475			if _, err := f.WriteString("\n"); err != nil {
476				f.Close()
477				os.Remove(tmp)
478				return fmt.Errorf("bead: write newline: %w", err)
479			}
480		}
481	
482		if err := f.Sync(); err != nil {
483			f.Close()
484			os.Remove(tmp)
485			return fmt.Errorf("bead: sync tmp: %w", err)
486		}
487		if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
488			os.Remove(tmp)
489			return fmt.Errorf("bead: close tmp: %w", err)
490		}
491		if err := os.Rename(tmp, s.File); err != nil {
492			os.Remove(tmp)
493			return fmt.Errorf("bead: rename tmp: %w", err)
494		}
495		return nil
496	}

498	// Create adds a new bead. Assigns defaults, validates, then persists.
499	func (s *Store) Create(ctx context.Context, b *Bead) error {
500		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
501			return err
502		}
503		if b == nil {
504			return fmt.Errorf("bead: create requires bead")
505		}
506		now := time.Now().UTC()
507		if b.ID == "" {
508			id, err := s.GenID(ctx)
509			if err != nil {
510				return err
511			}
512			b.ID = id
513		}
514		if b.IssueType == "" {
515			b.IssueType = DefaultType
516		}
517		if b.Status == "" {
518			// Create-time StatusOpen assignment is initial state, not a lifecycle transition.
519			// This is the sole exception to ValidateLifecycleTransition: all other status
520			// writes go through transitionLifecycleInPlace, which validates transitions via
521			// ValidateLifecycleTransition (TD-031 §3 transition matrix pattern).
522			b.Status = DefaultStatus
523		}
524		b.CreatedAt = now
525		b.UpdatedAt = now
526	
527		// Validate after defaults are applied so hooks see final state
528		if err := s.validateBead(b); err != nil {
529			return err
530		}
531		// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create another
532		// operator-prompt bead. The execute-bead harness exports the actor's
533		// issue_type via DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE; absent the env, the guard is a
534		// no-op (e.g. direct CLI use by a human operator).
535		if err := OperatorPromptMutationGuard(os.Getenv("DDX_ACTOR_ISSUE_TYPE"), b.IssueType); err != nil {
536			return err
537		}
538		// Run create hook
539		if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-create", b); err != nil {
540			return err
541		}
542	
543		return s.WithLock(func() error {
544			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
545			if err != nil {
546				return err
547			}
548			byID := make(map[string]*Bead, len(beads))
549			// Reject duplicate IDs
550			for i := range beads {
551				e := &beads[i]
552				byID[e.ID] = e
553				if e.ID == b.ID {
554					return fmt.Errorf("bead: duplicate id: %s", b.ID)
555				}
556			}
557			// Validate deps reference existing beads
558			depIDs := b.DepIDs()
559			if len(depIDs) > 0 {
560				existing := make(map[string]bool)
561				for _, e := range beads {
562					existing[e.ID] = true
563				}
564				for _, dep := range depIDs {
565					if !existing[dep] {
566						return fmt.Errorf("bead: dependency not found: %s", dep)
567					}
568				}
569			}
570			parentChain, err := beadParentChain(byID, b.Parent)
571			if err != nil {
572				return err
573			}
574			for _, depID := range depIDs {
575				if err := rejectAncestorDependency(b.ID, depID, parentChain); err != nil {
576					return err
577				}
578			}
579			beads = append(beads, *b)
580			return s.WriteAll(beads)
581		})
582	}

584	// Get retrieves a bead by ID.
585	func (s *Store) Get(ctx context.Context, id string) (*Bead, error) {
586		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
587			return nil, err
588		}
589		if id == "" {
590			return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: get requires bead id")
591		}
592		beads, err := s.ReadAll(ctx)
593		if err != nil {
594			return nil, err
595		}
596		for _, b := range beads {
597			if b.ID == id {
598				return &b, nil
599			}
600		}
601		return nil, fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
602	}

604	// Update modifies a bead by ID. The mutate function receives a pointer to
605	// modify, but persisted lifecycle status changes must use TransitionLifecycle.
606	func (s *Store) Update(ctx context.Context, id string, mutate func(*Bead)) error {
607		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
608			return err
609		}
610		if id == "" {
611			return fmt.Errorf("bead: update requires bead id")
612		}
613		if mutate == nil {
614			return fmt.Errorf("bead: update requires mutate func")
615		}
616		return s.updateBead(id, false, func(b *Bead) error {
617			mutate(b)
618			return nil
619		})
620	}

622	// Apply mutates a bead through a typed Operation. When the configured raw
623	// backend exposes the optional OperationApplier fast path, Apply delegates to
624	// it; otherwise it falls back to the generic load-mutate-save path.
625	func (s *Store) Apply(id string, op Operation) error {
626		if s == nil {
627			return fmt.Errorf("bead: nil store")
628		}
629		if op == nil {
630			return fmt.Errorf("bead: nil operation")
631		}
632		if applier, ok := s.backend.(OperationApplier); ok {
633			return applier.Apply(context.Background(), id, op)
634		}
635		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
636			return op.Apply(b)
637		})
638	}

640	func (s *Store) updateBead(id string, allowStatusChange bool, mutate func(*Bead) error) error {
641		return s.WithLock(func() error {
642			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
643			if err != nil {
644				return err
645			}
646			found := false
647			for i := range beads {
648				if beads[i].ID == id {
649					beforeStatus := beads[i].Status
650					beforeDeps := make(map[string]bool, len(beads[i].Dependencies))
651					for _, dep := range beads[i].DepIDs() {
652						beforeDeps[dep] = true
653					}
654					if mutate != nil {
655						if err := mutate(&beads[i]); err != nil {
656							return err
657						}
658					}
659					if !allowStatusChange && beads[i].Status != beforeStatus {
660						return fmt.Errorf("bead: lifecycle status change %s -> %s requires Store.TransitionLifecycle", beforeStatus, beads[i].Status)
661					}
662					beads[i].UpdatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
663					// Core validation after mutation
664					if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
665						return err
666					}
667					// Reject NEWLY-introduced dependency edges whose target bead does
668					// not exist — these can never be satisfied and block the referrer
669					// from readiness forever (the phantom/dangling-dep class). Pre-
670					// existing dangling edges are grandfathered so an already-corrupted
671					// bead stays updatable and repairable via `ddx bead doctor --fix`.
672					if mutate != nil {
673						existing := make(map[string]bool, len(beads))
674						for j := range beads {
675							existing[beads[j].ID] = true
676						}
677						for _, dep := range beads[i].DepIDs() {
678							if existing[dep] || beforeDeps[dep] {
679								continue
680							}
681							return fmt.Errorf("bead: refusing to write dangling dependency edge: target %s does not exist", dep)
682						}
683					}
684					// Run update hook
685					if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
686						return err
687					}
688					found = true
689					break
690				}
691			}
692			if !found {
693				return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
694			}
695			return s.WriteAll(beads)
696		})
697	}

699	// TransitionLifecycle validates and persists one bead lifecycle status
700	// transition. Callers may provide a mutator for metadata that must land
701	// atomically with the status write.
702	func (s *Store) TransitionLifecycle(id string, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions, mutate func(*Bead) error) error {
703		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
704			beforeStatus := b.Status
705			if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, status, opts); err != nil {
706				return err
707			}
708			if mutate != nil {
709				return mutate(b)
710			}
711			appendOperatorAcceptedTriagedEventIfNeeded(b, beforeStatus, status, opts)
712			return nil
713		})
714	}

716	// SetLifecycleStatus validates and persists a lifecycle status transition.
717	func (s *Store) SetLifecycleStatus(id string, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) error {
718		return s.TransitionLifecycle(id, status, opts, nil)
719	}

721	// UpdateWithLifecycleStatus atomically applies non-status field mutations and
722	// a validated lifecycle status transition.
723	func (s *Store) UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(id string, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions, mutate func(*Bead) error) error {
724		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
725			beforeStatus := b.Status
726			if mutate != nil {
727				if err := mutate(b); err != nil {
728					return err
729				}
730			}
731			if b.Status != beforeStatus {
732				return fmt.Errorf("bead: UpdateWithLifecycleStatus mutator changed lifecycle status directly")
733			}
734			if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(b, status, opts); err != nil {
735				return err
736			}
737			appendOperatorAcceptedTriagedEventIfNeeded(b, beforeStatus, status, opts)
738			return nil
739		})
740	}
741	
742	func transitionLifecycleInPlace(b *Bead, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) error {
743		from := b.Status
744		if err := ValidateLifecycleTransition(from, status, opts); err != nil {
745			return fmt.Errorf("bead: lifecycle transition %s -> %s rejected: %w", from, status, err)
746		}
747		b.Status = status
748		applyLifecycleTransitionMetadata(b, from, status, opts)
749		return nil
750	}
751	
752	const ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason = "lifecycle-external-blocker-reason"
753	
754	// ExtraConsecutiveWedgeMarker is the bead Extra key holding the consecutive-wedge
755	// count recorded by the execute-bead loop. It is cleared at the store layer on
756	// any transition to StatusOpen so that operator-reopened beads get a fresh
757	// attempt rather than being instantly re-parked by the guard (ddx-5c549120).
758	const ExtraConsecutiveWedgeMarker = "work-consecutive-wedges"
759	
760	func applyLifecycleTransitionMetadata(b *Bead, from, status string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) {
761		if b.Extra == nil {
762			b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
763		}
764		if status == StatusBlocked {
765			if reason := strings.TrimSpace(opts.ExternalBlockerReason); reason != "" {
766				b.Extra[ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason] = reason
767			}
768		} else {
769			delete(b.Extra, ExtraLifecycleExternalBlockerReason)
770		}
771		// Clear stale consecutive-wedge marker on operator reopen (proposed/blocked →
772		// open) so the execute-bead loop gets a fresh attempt rather than re-parking
773		// without trying (ddx-5c549120). Only clears on operator-facing transitions;
774		// internal release (in_progress → open) preserves the count.
775		if status == StatusOpen && (from == StatusProposed || from == StatusBlocked) {
776			delete(b.Extra, ExtraConsecutiveWedgeMarker)
777		}
778	}
779	
780	func appendOperatorAcceptedTriagedEventIfNeeded(b *Bead, from, to string, opts LifecycleTransitionOptions) {
781		if b == nil || from != StatusProposed || to != StatusOpen {
782			return
783		}
784		acceptedFingerprint, acceptedPromptFingerprint := latestParkedIntakeFingerprint(b)
785		body := map[string]any{
786			"from_status":                 from,
787			"to_status":                   to,
788			"reason":                      strings.TrimSpace(opts.Reason),
789			"source":                      strings.TrimSpace(opts.Source),
790			"operator_required":           true,
791			"accepted_fingerprint":        acceptedFingerprint,
792			"accepted_prompt_fingerprint": acceptedPromptFingerprint,
793		}
794		if actor := strings.TrimSpace(opts.Actor); actor != "" {
795			body["actor"] = actor
796		}
797		if promptFingerprint := PromptFingerprint(*b); promptFingerprint != "" {
798			body["prompt_fingerprint"] = promptFingerprint
799		}
800		payload, _ := json.Marshal(body)
801		appendInlineEvent(b, BeadEvent{
802			Kind:      "triaged",
803			Summary:   "operator accepted proposed bead",
804			Body:      string(payload),
805			Actor:     strings.TrimSpace(opts.Actor),
806			Source:    strings.TrimSpace(opts.Source),
807			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
808		})

811	func latestParkedIntakeFingerprint(b *Bead) (string, string) {
812		if b == nil || b.Extra == nil {

814		}

819				fields := beadEventBodyFields(events[i].Body)

821				if fingerprint == "" {

824				return fingerprint, strings.TrimSpace(fields["prompt_fingerprint"])
825			}
826		}

830	func beadEventBodyFields(body string) map[string]string {
831		fields := map[string]string{}
832		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
833			return fields
834		}
835		var raw map[string]any
836		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(body), &raw); err != nil {
837			return fields
838		}
839		for k, v := range raw {
840			if s, ok := v.(string); ok {
841				fields[k] = s
842			}
843		}
844		return fields

852	// HeartbeatTTL is the maximum allowed age of the external claim liveness
853	// lease before another worker may reclaim the bead. Defaults to 3×
854	// HeartbeatInterval. Exposed as a variable so tests can override it.

857	// Claim sets a bead to in_progress with claim metadata.
858	// Fails if the bead is already claimed (status == in_progress) with a
859	// fresh claim lease. A bead whose external lease is older than HeartbeatTTL
860	// is considered stalled, except that a same-machine owner with a live PID is
861	// protected until the hard fallback ceiling expires.
862	func (s *Store) Claim(id, assignee string) error {
863		machine := currentMachineID()
864		return s.WithLock(func() error {
865			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
866			if err != nil {
867				return err
868			}
869			for i := range beads {
870				if beads[i].ID != id {
871					continue
872				}
873				switch beads[i].Status {
874				case StatusOpen:
875					if !claimLeaseIsStale(s, beads[i].Extra, id) {
876						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
877					}
878					// normal claim path
879				case StatusInProgress:
880					if !claimLeaseIsStale(s, beads[i].Extra, id) {
881						return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
882					}
883					// stalled claim — reclaim atomically below
884				default:
885					return fmt.Errorf("bead: cannot claim %s from status %s", id, beads[i].Status)
886				}
887				if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(&beads[i], StatusInProgress, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
888					Reason: "claim",
889					Actor:  assignee,
890					Source: "Store.Claim",
891				}); err != nil {
892					return err
893				}
894				now := time.Now().UTC()
895				beads[i].Owner = assignee
896				beads[i].UpdatedAt = now
897				if beads[i].Extra == nil {
898					beads[i].Extra = make(map[string]any)
899				}
900				// Compatibility cleanup only: worker/live claim metadata now lives in
901				// the external lease sidecar rather than the tracked row.
902				for _, key := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
903					delete(beads[i].Extra, key)
904				}
905				delete(beads[i].Extra, ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey)
906				if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
907					return err
908				}
909				if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
910					return err
911				}
912				if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
913					return err
914				}
915				return s.writeClaimHeartbeat(ClaimLeaseRecord{
916					BeadID:    id,
917					Owner:     assignee,
918					Machine:   machine,
919					StartedAt: now,
920					UpdatedAt: now,
921					PID:       os.Getpid(),
922				})
923			}
924			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
925		})
926	}

1001	// claimLeaseIsStale returns true if the external claim lease is absent or
1002	// older than HeartbeatTTL. Same-machine live owners remain protected until the
1003	// hard fallback ceiling expires. When no lease file exists, it falls back to
1004	// the legacy tracker heartbeat field for compatibility with imported/stale rows.
1005	func claimLeaseIsStale(s *Store, extra map[string]any, id string) bool {
1006		if s != nil {
1007			if rec, found, err := s.readClaimHeartbeat(id); err == nil {
1008				if found {
1009					return claimLeaseRecordIsStale(rec)
1010				}
1011			} else {
1012				return heartbeatIsStale(extra)
1013			}
1014		}
1015		return heartbeatIsStale(extra)
1016	}

1018	// heartbeatIsStale returns true if the given bead's legacy Extra map has no
1019	// heartbeat field or one older than HeartbeatTTL.
1020	func heartbeatIsStale(extra map[string]any) bool {
1021		if extra == nil {
1022			return true
1023		}
1024		raw, ok := extra["work-heartbeat-at"]
1025		if !ok {
1026			return true
1027		}
1028		s, ok := raw.(string)
1029		if !ok || s == "" {
1030			return true
1031		}
1032		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)
1033		if err != nil {
1034			// Fall back to RFC3339 for compatibility with legacy entries written
1035			// before sub-second resolution. RFC3339Nano is the current canonical
1036			// format used by both ClaimWithOptions and Heartbeat write sites.
1037			t, err = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s)
1038			if err != nil {
1039				return true
1040			}
1041		}
1042		return time.Since(t) > HeartbeatTTL
1043	}

1045	// clearClaimExtraKeys resets claim state by deleting all claim-related metadata

1047	// claimed-machine, claimed-session, claimed-worktree) and the claim heartbeat lease
1048	// timestamp. These keys are cleared when a bead is unclaimed or reopened to fully
1049	// reset its claim state so it can be claimed again by another worker.
1050	func clearClaimExtraKeys(extra map[string]any) {
1051		if extra == nil {
1052			return
1053		}
1054		for _, k := range ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
1055			delete(extra, k)
1056		}
1057		delete(extra, ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey)

1061	// is currently in_progress — a closed bead stays closed.

1066					Reason: "unclaim",
1067					Source: "Store.Unclaim",
1068				}); err != nil {
1069					return err
1070				}
1071			}
1072			b.Owner = ""
1073			clearClaimExtraKeys(b.Extra)
1074			return nil
1075		}); err != nil {
1076			return err
1077		}

1081	// Release is the atomic, symmetric counterpart to Claim: it transitions a
1082	// claimed bead back to targetStatus (StatusOpen when empty), clears Owner,
1083	// persists the row, AND removes the external claim-heartbeat lease sidecar —
1084	// all inside a single WithLock critical section. Unlike Unclaim (which removes
1085	// the lease only after the lock is released), Release couples claim clearing
1086	// and lifecycle status in one locked write, so a failure-release can never
1087	// leave a stale lease sidecar while `ddx bead status` reports the bead as not
1088	// in_progress (the phantom-lease inconsistency observed in ddx-8f2e0ebf).
1089	//
1090	// Only an in_progress bead has its status transitioned; a bead in any other
1091	// status (e.g. closed) keeps its status and only has Owner and the lease
1092	// cleared, mirroring Unclaim's guard so a released bead is never reopened.
1093	func (s *Store) Release(id, assignee, targetStatus string) error {
1094		if strings.TrimSpace(targetStatus) == "" {
1095			targetStatus = StatusOpen
1096		}
1097		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1098			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1099			if err != nil {
1100				return err
1101			}
1102			for i := range beads {
1103				if beads[i].ID != id {
1104					continue
1105				}
1106				if beads[i].Status == StatusInProgress {
1107					if err := transitionLifecycleInPlace(&beads[i], targetStatus, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1108						Reason: "release",
1109						Actor:  assignee,
1110						Source: "Store.Release",
1111					}); err != nil {
1112						return err
1113					}
1114				}
1115				beads[i].Owner = ""
1116				beads[i].UpdatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
1117				clearClaimExtraKeys(beads[i].Extra)
1118				if err := s.validateBead(&beads[i]); err != nil {
1119					return err
1120				}
1121				if err := s.runHook("validate-bead-update", &beads[i]); err != nil {
1122					return err
1123				}
1124				if err := s.WriteAll(beads); err != nil {
1125					return err
1126				}
1127				return s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1128			}
1129			return fmt.Errorf("bead: not found: %s", id)
1130		})
1131	}

1133	// SetExecutionCooldown parks bead id until the given wall-clock time.
1134	// baseRev is the origin/main HEAD at cooldown time; when non-empty it enables
1135	// rev-bound auto-invalidation: if origin/main advances past baseRev the
1136	// cooldown clears automatically on the next ready-queue pass.
1137	func (s *Store) SetExecutionCooldown(id string, until time.Time, status, detail, baseRev string) error {
1138		return s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1139			if b.Extra == nil {
1140				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1141			}
1142			b.Extra["work-retry-after"] = until.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
1143			if status != "" {
1144				b.Extra["work-last-status"] = status
1145			}
1146			if detail != "" {
1147				b.Extra["work-last-detail"] = detail
1148			}
1149			if baseRev != "" {
1150				b.Extra[ExtraCooldownBaseRev] = baseRev
1151			} else {
1152				delete(b.Extra, ExtraCooldownBaseRev)
1153			}
1154		})
1155	}

1157	// ClearCooldowns clears work cooldown fields for all beads matched by
1158	// filter. If filter is nil, every bead with work-retry-after set is
1159	// cleared. Returns the count of beads whose cooldowns were cleared. A
1160	// "cooldown-cleared" event with reason="operator-bulk-clear" is appended for
1161	// each cleared bead in the same pass.
1162	func (s *Store) ClearCooldowns(filter func(Bead) bool) (int, error) {
1163		var count int
1164		err := s.WithLock(func() error {
1165			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1166			if err != nil {
1167				return err
1168			}
1169			now := time.Now().UTC()
1170			for i := range beads {
1171				b := &beads[i]
1172				if extraStringVal(b.Extra, ExtraRetryAfter) == "" {
1173					continue
1174				}
1175				if filter != nil && !filter(*b) {
1176					continue
1177				}
1178				if b.Extra == nil {
1179					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1180				}
1181				delete(b.Extra, ExtraRetryAfter)
1182				delete(b.Extra, ExtraCooldownBaseRev)
1183				delete(b.Extra, ExtraLastStatus)
1184				delete(b.Extra, ExtraLastDetail)
1185				b.UpdatedAt = now
1186				var events []BeadEvent
1187				if raw, ok := b.Extra["events"]; ok {
1188					events = decodeBeadEvents(raw)
1189				}
1190				events = append(events, BeadEvent{
1191					Kind:      "cooldown-cleared",
1192					Summary:   "operator-bulk-clear",
1193					Source:    "ddx work clear-cooldowns",
1194					CreatedAt: now,
1195				})
1196				b.Extra["events"] = encodeEventsForExtra(events)
1197				if err := s.validateBead(b); err != nil {
1198					return err
1199				}
1200				count++
1201			}
1202			if count == 0 {
1203				return nil
1204			}
1205			return s.WriteAll(beads)
1206		})
1207		return count, err
1208	}

1237	// MaxFieldBytes is the per-field hard cap on bead event bodies and adjacent
1238	// writer paths. 65,535 bytes matches upstream bd's Dolt TEXT column size so
1239	// DDx-authored beads always round-trip through `bd import`. Empirically
1240	// validated against bd 1.0.2 on 2026-04-20: 65,535 accepts, 65,536 rejects.
1241	const MaxFieldBytes = 65535
1242	
1243	// capFieldBytes enforces MaxFieldBytes on a single field value. Callers that
1244	// need to preserve the full payload should persist it to a sidecar artifact
1245	// and store a path reference in the field; this function is the defense-in-
1246	// depth cap for code paths that don't. Returns the input unchanged when it
1247	// fits; otherwise returns head+tail truncation with a byte-count marker.
1248	func capFieldBytes(s string) string {
1249		if len(s) <= MaxFieldBytes {
1250			return s
1251		}
1252		// Reserve a marker that always fits; keep head heavy (2/3) so
1253		// human-readable context is preserved for the common short-rationale case.
1254		marker := fmt.Sprintf("\n…[truncated, %d bytes]\n", len(s))
1255		budget := MaxFieldBytes - len(marker)
1256		if budget <= 0 {
1257			return s[:MaxFieldBytes]
1258		}
1259		head := (budget * 2) / 3
1260		tail := budget - head
1261		return s[:head] + marker + s[len(s)-tail:]
1262	}

1264	// Cancel-marker keys on Bead.Extra. ADR-022 §Cancel SLA: an operator-initiated
1265	// cancel writes cancel-requested:true; the worker that honors the cancel writes
1266	// cancel-honored:true alongside it.
1267	const (
1268		ExtraCancelRequested = "cancel-requested"
1269		ExtraCancelHonored   = "cancel-honored"
1270	)
1271	
1272	// RequestCancel writes Extra[cancel-requested]=true on the bead. Idempotent:
1273	// if cancel-honored is already set the call is a silent no-op (the prior cancel
1274	// was already consumed). Returns true when the marker is now set on the bead
1275	// (either by this call or a prior one).
1276	func (s *Store) RequestCancel(id string) (bool, error) {
1277		var set bool
1278		err := s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1279			if b.Extra == nil {
1280				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1281			}
1282			if isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelHonored]) {
1283				set = isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelRequested])
1284				return
1285			}
1286			b.Extra[ExtraCancelRequested] = true
1287			set = true
1288		})
1289		return set, err
1290	}
1291	
1292	// IsCancelRequested reports whether the bead carries an unconsumed cancel
1293	// marker (cancel-requested:true and cancel-honored not yet set).
1294	func (s *Store) IsCancelRequested(id string) (bool, error) {
1295		b, err := s.Get(context.Background(), id)
1296		if err != nil {
1297			return false, err
1298		}
1299		if b == nil || b.Extra == nil {
1300			return false, nil
1301		}
1302		if isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelHonored]) {
1303			return false, nil
1304		}
1305		return isExtraTrue(b.Extra[ExtraCancelRequested]), nil
1306	}

1308	// MarkCancelHonored sets Extra[cancel-honored]=true. Called by the worker once
1309	// it has aborted at the next safe point in response to a cancel request.

1312			if b.Extra == nil {
1313				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1314			}
1315			b.Extra[ExtraCancelHonored] = true
1316		})
1317	}

1319	func isExtraTrue(v any) bool {
1320		switch x := v.(type) {
1321		case bool:
1322			return x
1323		case string:
1324			return x == "true"
1325		}
1326		return false

1329	func (s *Store) AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error {
1330		if event.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
1331			event.CreatedAt = time.Now().UTC()
1332		}
1333		// Defense in depth: cap every event body regardless of caller. The
1334		// reviewer write path separately persists the full stream to an artifact
1335		// and emits a <=512-byte body; this cap catches anything else.
1336		event.Body = capFieldBytes(event.Body)
1337		event.Summary = capFieldBytes(event.Summary)
1338		// Two storage shapes are possible here: inline Extra["events"] for active
1339		// beads, and a sidecar attachment for closed/archived beads (ADR-004
1340		// attachment model). When the bead has been externalized we append to the
1341		// sidecar and leave the row untouched aside from updated_at.
1342		var appendedToAttachment bool
1343		if err := s.Update(context.Background(), id, func(b *Bead) {
1344			if b.Extra == nil {
1345				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1346			}
1347			if hasEventsAttachment(b) {
1348				appendedToAttachment = true
1349				return
1350			}
1351			var events []BeadEvent
1352			if raw, ok := b.Extra["events"]; ok {
1353				events = decodeBeadEvents(raw)
1354			}
1355			events = append(events, event)
1356			b.Extra["events"] = encodeEventsForExtra(events)
1357		}); err != nil {
1358			return err
1359		}
1360		if !appendedToAttachment {
1361			return nil
1362		}
1363		// Append to the sidecar under the store lock so concurrent readers and
1364		// writers see a consistent events log.
1365		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1366			existing, err := s.readEventsAttachment(id)
1367			if err != nil {
1368				return err
1369			}
1370			existing = append(existing, event)
1371			return s.writeEventsAttachment(id, existing)
1372		})
1373	}

1375	// Events returns the bead's evidence history in insertion order. Reads are
1376	// transparent across storage shapes: inline Extra["events"] for active beads,
1377	// or the externalized sidecar referenced by Extra[events_attachment] once the
1378	// bead has been closed.
1379	func (s *Store) Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error) {
1380		b, err := s.Get(context.Background(), id)
1381		if err != nil {
1382			return nil, err
1383		}
1384		events, err := s.eventsForBead(b)
1385		if err != nil {
1386			return nil, err
1387		}
1388		if len(events) == 0 {
1389			return []BeadEvent{}, nil
1390		}
1391		out := make([]BeadEvent, len(events))
1392		copy(out, events)
1393		return out, nil
1394	}

1396	// EventsByKind returns all events for a bead filtered by kind, in insertion order.
1397	func (s *Store) EventsByKind(id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error) {
1398		all, err := s.Events(id)
1399		if err != nil {
1400			return nil, err
1401		}
1402		out := make([]BeadEvent, 0, len(all))
1403		for _, e := range all {
1404			if e.Kind == kind {
1405				out = append(out, e)
1406			}
1407		}
1408		return out, nil
1409	}

1411	func decodeBeadEvents(raw any) []BeadEvent {
1412		switch v := raw.(type) {
1413		case []BeadEvent:
1414			out := make([]BeadEvent, len(v))
1415			copy(out, v)
1416			return out
1417		case []any:
1418			out := make([]BeadEvent, 0, len(v))
1419			for _, item := range v {
1420				m, ok := item.(map[string]any)
1421				if !ok {
1422					continue
1423				}
1424				out = append(out, beadEventFromMap(m))
1425			}
1426			return out
1427		case []map[string]any:
1428			out := make([]BeadEvent, 0, len(v))
1429			for _, item := range v {
1430				out = append(out, beadEventFromMap(item))
1431			}
1432			return out
1433		default:
1434			return nil
1435		}
1436	}
1437	
1438	func beadEventFromMap(m map[string]any) BeadEvent {
1439		e := BeadEvent{}
1440		if v, ok := m["kind"].(string); ok {
1441			e.Kind = v
1442		}
1443		if v, ok := m["summary"].(string); ok {
1444			e.Summary = v
1445		}
1446		if v, ok := m["body"].(string); ok {
1447			e.Body = v
1448		}
1449		if v, ok := m["actor"].(string); ok {
1450			e.Actor = v
1451		}
1452		if v, ok := m["source"].(string); ok {
1453			e.Source = v
1454		}
1455		if v, ok := m["created_at"].(string); ok {
1456			if parsed, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, v); err == nil {
1457				e.CreatedAt = parsed
1458			}
1459		}
1460		return e
1461	}

1463	// lastReviewVerdictFromEvents returns the verdict string from the last
1464	// kind:review event in the list. Returns "" when no review event exists.
1465	func lastReviewVerdictFromEvents(events []BeadEvent) string {
1466		verdict := ""
1467		for _, e := range events {
1468			if e.Kind != "review" {
1469				continue
1470			}
1471			v := strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(e.Summary))
1472			switch v {
1473			case "APPROVE", "BLOCK", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "REQUEST_CLARIFICATION":
1474				verdict = v
1475			}
1476		}
1477		return verdict
1478	}

1480	// Close sets a bead's status to closed. When the close succeeds and the bead
1481	// carries an inline event history, those events are moved to a sidecar
1482	// attachment under .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl so the active row stays
1483	// small (per ADR-004's attachment model and TD-027 §c).
1484	func (s *Store) Close(ctx context.Context, id string) error {
1485		if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
1486			return err
1487		}
1488		if id == "" {
1489			return fmt.Errorf("bead: close requires bead id")
1490		}
1491		// Capture last review verdict before closure for reviewer-accuracy tracking.
1492		// If the operator manually closes a BLOCK-reviewed bead, that contradicts
1493		// the reviewer's verdict (potential false-positive).
1494		verdictWas := ""
1495		if events, err := s.Events(id); err == nil {
1496			verdictWas = lastReviewVerdictFromEvents(events)
1497		}
1498	
1499		if err := s.SetLifecycleStatus(id, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1500			ManualClose: true,
1501			Reason:      "manual close",
1502			Source:      "Store.Close",
1503		}); err != nil {
1504			return err
1505		}
1506		if err := s.externalizeEvents(id); err != nil {
1507			return err
1508		}
1509		// Emit accuracy override when operator closes a BLOCK-reviewed bead.
1510		// AppendEvent handles sidecar append for already-externalized events.
1511		if verdictWas == "BLOCK" {
1512			_ = s.AppendEvent(id, BeadEvent{
1513				Kind:      "review-accuracy-override",
1514				Summary:   "BLOCK verdict contradicted by operator close",
1515				Body:      "verdict_was=BLOCK\noperator_action=close\nreason=manual close",
1516				Source:    "Store.Close",
1517				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1518			})
1519		}
1520		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1521		s.maybeOpportunisticMaintenance()
1522	
1523		// Cascade-close eligible beads superseded by X, then walk up one hop
1524		// to close dead-intermediate (execution-eligible==false) beads.
1525		// Both operations share a visited set to prevent cycles within Close().
1526		visited := map[string]bool{id: true}
1527		_ = s.cascadeAndWalkUp(id, visited)
1528	
1529		return nil
1530	}
1531	
1532	// externalizeEvents moves a bead's inline events into its sidecar attachment
1533	// under a fresh lock. Safe to call repeatedly — no-op when there are no
1534	// inline events (already externalized, or none recorded).
1535	func (s *Store) externalizeEvents(id string) error {
1536		if s.axonGraphQLActive() {
1537			return nil
1538		}
1539		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1540			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1541			if err != nil {
1542				return err
1543			}
1544			for i := range beads {
1545				if beads[i].ID != id {
1546					continue
1547				}
1548				if err := s.externalizeEventsInPlace(&beads[i]); err != nil {
1549					return err
1550				}
1551				return s.WriteAll(beads)
1552			}
1553			return nil
1554		})
1555	}

1557	// cascadeAndWalkUp performs both cascade-close (RC-2) and walk-up (RC-3) cleanup
1558	// within a single Close() invocation, sharing the visited set to prevent cycles.
1559	// First cascades close beads superseded by closedID, then walks up one hop to
1560	// check if the closed bead's parent should be auto-closed as a dead-intermediate.
1561	func (s *Store) cascadeAndWalkUp(closedID string, visited map[string]bool) error {
1562		all, err := s.ReadAll(context.Background())
1563		if err != nil {
1564			return err
1565		}
1566	
1567		// RC-2: Cascade-close superseded beads
1568		for _, x := range all {
1569			if visited[x.ID] {
1570				continue
1571			}
1572			if !s.canCascadeCloseSuperseeded(&x, closedID, all) {
1573				continue
1574			}
1575			visited[x.ID] = true
1576			_ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(x.ID, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1577				ManualClose: true,
1578				Reason:      fmt.Sprintf("cascade-close superseded by %s", closedID),
1579				Source:      "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1580			})
1581			_ = s.externalizeEvents(x.ID)
1582			_ = s.AppendEvent(x.ID, BeadEvent{
1583				Kind:      "superseded_close",
1584				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("closed as superseded via %s", closedID),
1585				Body:      fmt.Sprintf("closed_by_cascade_of: %s\nreason: closed_as_superseded_via:%s", closedID, closedID),
1586				Source:    "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1587				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1588			})
1589			_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(x.ID)
1590		}
1591	
1592		// RC-3: Walk up the parent chain to close dead-intermediate
1593		// (execution-eligible==false) beads and nested epic ancestors.
1594		closedBead := findBeadByID(all, closedID)
1595		if closedBead != nil && closedBead.Parent != "" {
1596			if !visited[closedBead.Parent] {
1597				s.walkUpClosureCandidate(closedBead.Parent, all, visited)
1598			}
1599		}
1600	
1601		return nil
1602	}
1603	
1604	// walkUpClosureCandidate checks if parent should be auto-closed when all its
1605	// children reach terminal state. Two closure paths:
1606	//   - Dead-intermediate: parent has execution-eligible=false; auto-closes once
1607	//     all children are terminal (RC-3 walk-up closure).
1608	//   - Epic auto-close: parent is an epic bead; auto-closes when every child is
1609	//     closed or cancelled (both count as terminal).
1610	//
1611	// Cancelled children are treated as terminal for both paths.
1612	func (s *Store) walkUpClosureCandidate(parentID string, allBeads []Bead, visited map[string]bool) {
1613		parent := findBeadByID(allBeads, parentID)
1614		if parent == nil {
1615			return
1616		}
1617	
1618		// Count children; closed AND cancelled both count as terminal.
1619		nonTerminalChildCount := 0
1620		closedChildCount := 0
1621		totalChildCount := 0
1622		for _, b := range allBeads {
1623			if b.Parent == parentID {
1624				totalChildCount++
1625				if b.Status == StatusClosed {
1626					closedChildCount++
1627				} else if b.Status != StatusCancelled {
1628					nonTerminalChildCount++
1629				}
1630			}
1631		}
1632	
1633		if nonTerminalChildCount > 0 || totalChildCount == 0 {
1634			return
1635		}
1636	
1637		executionEligible, executionEligibleKnown := lifecycleExecutionEligible(*parent)
1638		isDeadIntermediate := executionEligibleKnown && !executionEligible
1639		isEpic := isEpicBead(*parent)
1640	
1641		if !isDeadIntermediate && !isEpic {
1642			return
1643		}
1644	
1645		var eventKind, reason, summary, body string
1646		targetStatus := StatusClosed
1647		if isDeadIntermediate {
1648			reason = "auto-close dead-intermediate (all children closed, not execution-eligible)"
1649			eventKind = "dead_intermediate_close"
1650			summary = "closed as dead-intermediate bead (all children closed, execution-eligible=false)"
1651			body = fmt.Sprintf("closed_because: all_children_closed\nexecution_eligible: false\ntotal_children: %d", totalChildCount)
1652		} else if closedChildCount == 0 {
1653			// All children are cancelled — no work actually completed; cancel the epic.
1654			targetStatus = StatusCancelled
1655			reason = "auto-cancel epic: all children cancelled, no work completed"
1656			eventKind = "epic_auto_close"
1657			summary = "auto-cancelled: all children cancelled, no work completed"
1658			body = fmt.Sprintf("closed_because: all_children_cancelled\ntotal_children: %d", totalChildCount)
1659		} else {
1660			reason = "auto-close epic: all children reached terminal state"
1661			eventKind = "epic_auto_close"
1662			summary = "auto-closed: all children reached terminal state"
1663			body = fmt.Sprintf("closed_because: all_children_terminal\ntotal_children: %d", totalChildCount)
1664		}
1665	
1666		visited[parentID] = true
1667		_ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(parentID, targetStatus, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1668			ManualClose: true,
1669			Reason:      reason,
1670			Source:      "Store.Close.walkUpClosureCandidate",
1671		})
1672		_ = s.externalizeEvents(parentID)
1673		_ = s.AppendEvent(parentID, BeadEvent{
1674			Kind:      eventKind,
1675			Summary:   summary,
1676			Body:      body,
1677			Source:    "Store.Close.walkUpClosureCandidate",
1678			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1679		})
1680		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(parentID)
1681	
1682		if updated := findBeadByID(allBeads, parentID); updated != nil {
1683			updated.Status = targetStatus
1684		}
1685	
1686		if parent.Parent != "" && !visited[parent.Parent] {
1687			s.walkUpClosureCandidate(parent.Parent, allBeads, visited)
1688		}
1689	}
1690	
1691	// EpicClosureCandidates returns open epic beads whose children have all reached
1692	// a terminal state (closed or cancelled). Useful for backfill-closing epics that
1693	// predate auto-close, via ddx bead reap --apply.
1694	func (s *Store) EpicClosureCandidates(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error) {
1695		all, err := s.ReadAll(ctx)
1696		if err != nil {
1697			return nil, err
1698		}
1699		childCount := make(map[string]int)
1700		nonTerminalCount := make(map[string]int)
1701		for _, b := range all {
1702			if b.Parent == "" {
1703				continue
1704			}
1705			childCount[b.Parent]++
1706			if b.Status != StatusClosed && b.Status != StatusCancelled {
1707				nonTerminalCount[b.Parent]++
1708			}
1709		}
1710		var candidates []Bead
1711		for _, b := range all {
1712			if b.Status == StatusClosed || b.Status == StatusCancelled {
1713				continue
1714			}
1715			if childCount[b.ID] == 0 {
1716				continue
1717			}
1718			if nonTerminalCount[b.ID] > 0 {
1719				continue
1720			}
1721			if !isEpicBead(b) {
1722				continue
1723			}
1724			candidates = append(candidates, b)
1725		}
1726		return candidates, nil
1727	}

1729	// cascadeCloseSuperseeded scans for open beads X where superseded-by==Y,
1730	// checks strict scope guards, and closes eligible ones. One-hop deep only;
1731	// if X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's closure is deferred.
1732	// Idempotent: re-running is a no-op for already-closed X.
1733	func (s *Store) cascadeCloseSuperseeded(closedID string) error {
1734		all, err := s.ReadAll(context.Background())
1735		if err != nil {
1736			return err
1737		}
1738		visited := map[string]bool{closedID: true}
1739		for _, x := range all {
1740			if visited[x.ID] {
1741				continue
1742			}
1743			if !s.canCascadeCloseSuperseeded(&x, closedID, all) {
1744				continue
1745			}
1746			visited[x.ID] = true
1747			_ = s.SetLifecycleStatus(x.ID, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1748				ManualClose: true,
1749				Reason:      fmt.Sprintf("cascade-close superseded by %s", closedID),
1750				Source:      "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1751			})
1752			_ = s.externalizeEvents(x.ID)
1753			_ = s.AppendEvent(x.ID, BeadEvent{
1754				Kind:      "superseded_close",
1755				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("closed as superseded via %s", closedID),
1756				Body:      fmt.Sprintf("closed_by_cascade_of: %s\nreason: closed_as_superseded_via:%s", closedID, closedID),
1757				Source:    "Store.Close.cascadeCloseSuperseeded",
1758				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
1759			})
1760			_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(x.ID)
1761		}
1762		return nil
1763	}

1765	// canCascadeCloseSuperseeded checks all scope guards before cascade-closing X.
1766	// All guards must pass for a bead to be eligible. Reads current state fresh.
1767	func (s *Store) canCascadeCloseSuperseeded(x *Bead, closedID string, allBeads []Bead) bool {
1768		// Re-read X's current state to ensure fresh snapshot
1769		fresh, err := s.Get(context.Background(), x.ID)
1770		if err != nil {
1771			return false
1772		}
1773	
1774		// Guard 1: X is open AND (X.superseded-by == closedID OR X carries label superseded-by:closedID) AND closedID is closed
1775		if fresh.Status != StatusOpen {
1776			return false
1777		}
1778		superseededBy := lifecycleSupersededBy(*fresh)
1779		if superseededBy != closedID {
1780			found := false
1781			for _, l := range fresh.Labels {
1782				if l == fmt.Sprintf("superseded-by:%s", closedID) {
1783					found = true
1784					break
1785				}
1786			}
1787			if !found {
1788				return false
1789			}
1790		}
1791		// Verify closedID is actually closed
1792		var closedBead *Bead
1793		for i, b := range allBeads {
1794			if b.ID == closedID {
1795				closedBead = &allBeads[i]
1796				break
1797			}
1798		}
1799		if closedBead == nil || closedBead.Status != StatusClosed {
1800			return false
1801		}
1802	
1803		// Guard 2: X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X)
1804		for _, z := range allBeads {
1805			if z.Status == StatusOpen && z.ID != fresh.ID {
1806				for _, d := range z.Dependencies {
1807					if d.DependsOnID == fresh.ID {
1808						return false
1809					}
1810				}
1811			}
1812		}
1813	
1814		// Guard 3: X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker
1815		for _, d := range fresh.Dependencies {
1816			target := findBeadByID(allBeads, d.DependsOnID)
1817			if target != nil && target.Status != StatusClosed && target.Status != StatusCancelled {
1818				return false
1819			}
1820		}
1821	
1822		// Guard 4: X has attempt_count == 0 (no execution-related events)
1823		events, _ := s.Events(fresh.ID)
1824		for _, e := range events {
1825			if strings.Contains(e.Kind, "execution") || strings.Contains(e.Kind, "dispatch") ||
1826				strings.Contains(e.Kind, "work") || e.Kind == "execute-bead" {
1827				return false
1828			}
1829		}
1830	
1831		// Guard 5: X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp
1832		// For simplicity, check if notes are present (indicates operator activity)
1833		if strings.TrimSpace(fresh.Notes) != "" {
1834			return false
1835		}
1836	
1837		// Guard 6: X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id)
1838		if fresh.Status == StatusInProgress {
1839			return false
1840		}
1841		if claimFresh, _, err := s.ClaimHeartbeatFresh(fresh.ID); err == nil && claimFresh {
1842			return false
1843		}
1844	
1845		// Guard 7: X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label
1846		for _, l := range fresh.Labels {
1847			switch l {
1848			case "manual-hold", "no-auto-close", "container":
1849				return false
1850			}
1851		}
1852	
1853		return true
1854	}
1855	
1856	func findBeadByID(beads []Bead, id string) *Bead {
1857		for i, b := range beads {
1858			if b.ID == id {
1859				return &beads[i]
1860			}
1861		}
1862		return nil
1863	}
1864	
1865	// ErrClosureGateRejected indicates a close was refused because the bead does
1866	// not carry the evidence required for an automated closure: a terminal
1867	// verdict event (review APPROVE with non-empty rationale, or an explicit
1868	// review-skipped / manual-close marker) AND an execution-evidence marker
1869	// (closing_commit_sha, session_id, or an execute-bead success event in the
1870	// events history).
1871	//
1872	// Automated work paths (execute-bead + reviewer) always go through
1873	// CloseWithEvidence. The plain Store.Close remains as the manual
1874	// administration escape hatch — it bypasses the gate by design (ddx-e30e60a9
1875	// acceptance §1).
1876	var ErrClosureGateRejected = fmt.Errorf("closure gate: insufficient evidence")
1877	
1878	// ClosureGate inspects a bead and returns nil iff the close is safe. It
1879	// rejects only the specific shapes that caused the 2026-04-18/20
1880	// review-malfunction incidents:
1881	//
1882	//  1. axon-c5cc071a (silent false-closure): closed with no events AND no
1883	//     closing_commit_sha. Rejected by the execution-evidence check —
1884	//     closing_commit_sha must be non-empty OR at least one event must exist.
1885	//  2. f7ae036f (broken APPROVE): a review event with summary=APPROVE whose
1886	//     body is empty. Rejected — APPROVE with no rationale is exactly the
1887	//     parse-mis-extract shape the reviewer bug produced.
1888	//
1889	// Beads without a review step (--no-review, no Reviewer configured, already-
1890	// satisfied paths) pass the gate as long as they carry execution evidence.
1891	// This keeps the surface small: the gate catches known-bad shapes, not every
1892	// conceivable edge case. Additional invariants belong in future dedicated
1893	// beads so the rejection reason stays auditable.
1894	func ClosureGate(b *Bead) error {
1895		if b == nil {
1896			return fmt.Errorf("%w: nil bead", ErrClosureGateRejected)
1897		}
1898		hasClosingCommit := false
1899		if sha, ok := b.Extra["closing_commit_sha"].(string); ok && strings.TrimSpace(sha) != "" {
1900			hasClosingCommit = true
1901		}
1902		events := decodeBeadEvents(b.Extra["events"])
1903		// Externalized events still count as evidence for the gate. We only need
1904		// to know whether *some* event history exists, not iterate it; the
1905		// gate's APPROVE-with-empty-rationale check below relies on inline events
1906		// (the verdict is recorded before externalization).
1907		hasAttachedEvents := hasEventsAttachment(b)
1908		// Reject the axon-c5cc071a shape: no events AND no closing commit.
1909		if len(events) == 0 && !hasAttachedEvents && !hasClosingCommit {
1910			return fmt.Errorf("%w: no execution evidence (empty events and no closing_commit_sha)", ErrClosureGateRejected)
1911		}
1912		// Reject the f7ae036f shape: an APPROVE verdict with empty rationale.
1913		for _, e := range events {
1914			if e.Kind == "review" && strings.EqualFold(strings.TrimSpace(e.Summary), "APPROVE") {
1915				if strings.TrimSpace(e.Body) == "" {
1916					return fmt.Errorf("%w: review APPROVE with empty rationale (malformed reviewer verdict)", ErrClosureGateRejected)
1917				}
1918			}
1919		}
1920		return nil
1921	}
1922	
1923	// CloseWithEvidence sets a bead's status to closed and records agent session evidence.
1924	// sessionID is the agent session that completed the work.
1925	// commitSHA is the exact closing commit SHA when it is known.
1926	//
1927	// Enforces ClosureGate (ddx-e30e60a9): a bead cannot transition to closed
1928	// via this path without a terminal verdict event plus execution evidence.
1929	// Store.Close is the manual-administration escape hatch when the gate is
1930	// inappropriate.
1931	func (s *Store) CloseWithEvidence(id string, sessionID string, commitSHA string) error {
1932		if err := s.closeWithEvidence(id, sessionID, commitSHA); err != nil {
1933			return err
1934		}
1935		// Externalize events only when the gate actually transitioned the bead
1936		// to closed; rejected closes leave the bead open and inline.
1937		if b, err := s.Get(context.Background(), id); err == nil && b != nil && b.Status == StatusClosed {
1938			if err := s.externalizeEvents(id); err != nil {
1939				return err
1940			}
1941			// Walk up one hop to auto-close parent epics and dead-intermediates,
1942			// matching the cascade that Store.Close runs.
1943			visited := map[string]bool{id: true}
1944			_ = s.cascadeAndWalkUp(id, visited)
1945		}
1946		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1947		s.maybeOpportunisticMaintenance()
1948		return nil
1949	}
1950	
1951	func (s *Store) closeWithEvidence(id string, sessionID string, commitSHA string) error {
1952		return s.updateBead(id, true, func(b *Bead) error {
1953			if b.Extra == nil {
1954				b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
1955			}
1956			if sessionID != "" {
1957				b.Extra["session_id"] = sessionID
1958			}
1959			if commitSHA != "" {

ID:       ddx-3af1c1a6
Title:    D: Dependencies (ctx) + Queue helpers + Operations + remove 3 *Store queue methods (TD-027 §6, §14)
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:bead, area:dep, area:workflow, kind:refactor, phase:iterate, spec:TD-027
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-8d747049
Desc:     # PROBLEM
`*Store` exposes 3 dependency-graph methods (`DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree`) and 3 queue-ordering methods (`QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`). Per TD-027 §6 the dep methods belong on `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` sub-interfaces (signature-only change — ctx added). Queue ordering is operator-driven workflow; per TD-027 §14 it belongs in `ops/queue/` helpers with typed `Operation` values.

# ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/store.go` exposes these 6 methods. Foundation declared the `BeadDependencyReader`/`BeadDependencyWriter` interfaces but ctx isn't yet on `*Store`'s dep methods. `BeadQueue` isn't an interface in TD-027 — queue ops are workflow concerns that live as helpers.

# PROPOSED FIX

## Dependencies (signature-only)

1. Update `*Store.DepAdd(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepRemove(ctx, id, depID)`, `DepTree(ctx, rootID)` to take ctx.
2. Compile-time assertions: `var _ BeadDependencyReader = (*Store)(nil)`, `var _ BeadDependencyWriter = (*Store)(nil)`.
3. Thread ctx through callers. Narrow field types opportunistically (e.g., `ddx bead dep tree` CLI handler can narrow to `BeadDependencyReader`).

## Queue (helper-migration; methods removed)

4. Add named `Operation` types to `cli/internal/bead/operation.go`:
   - `QueueSetTop{}` — move bead to top of queue (sparse rank ordering).
   - `QueueSetPosition{Position int}` — operator move-to-position.
   - `QueueClearOp{}` — remove queue rank.
5. Create `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/queue.go`:
   - `Top(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueSetTop{}`.
   - `Move(ctx, l, id, position int) error` — wraps `QueueSetPosition`.
   - `Clear(ctx, l, id) error` — wraps `QueueClearOp{}`.
6. Migrate callers (operator queue manipulation in `cli/cmd/bead.go` queue subcommands) from `*Store.QueueTop`/`QueueMove`/`QueueClear` → `ops/queue/` helpers.
7. Remove `*Store.QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`.

# NON-SCOPE
- Dependency cycle detection — handled at higher layer.
- Queue rank renormalization algorithm — internal to ops/queue; sparse-rank logic remains as-is.
- Other concerns — separate beads.
- Module boundary lockdown — `ddx-74452926`.

# Acceptance criteria

1. `*Store.DepAdd`, `DepRemove`, `DepTree` take ctx. Compile-time assertions for `BeadDependencyReader` and `BeadDependencyWriter` compile.
2. `cli/internal/bead/operation.go` declares `QueueSetTop`, `QueueSetPosition{Position}`, `QueueClearOp` types with `Apply(b *Bead) error` impls. Tests: `TestOperation_QueueSetTop_AssignsTopRank`, `TestOperation_QueueSetPosition_RespectsBucket`, `TestOperation_QueueClearOp_RemovesRank`.
3. `cli/internal/bead/ops/queue/` package exists with helpers + tests: `TestOpsQueue_Top_MovesBeadToFront`, `TestOpsQueue_Move_PreservesPriorityBucket`, `TestOpsQueue_Clear_RemovesOperatorOverride`.
4. `*Store` no longer exposes `QueueTop`, `QueueMove`, `QueueClear`. Verified by grep.
5. Caller files no longer reference these methods on `*Store` outside the bead package.
6. Queue-ordering invariants (SD-004 §"Ready queue" / queue derivation) preserved — operator overrides apply within a priority bucket; explicit rank wins over missing rank within bucket. Test: `TestQueueOrdering_OperatorRankWithinBucket`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` is green.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then

go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Scan for secrets"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
        else
          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')

          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
            echo "Potential secrets detected"
            exit 1
          fi

    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"

        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
